name: Walnut Pepper Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl.
2. Add pepper to the dry ingredients and mix well.
3. In another bowl, cream butter with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
4. Gradually add sugar to the butter mixture and beat until fluffy.
5. Mix in eggs one at a time.
6. Stir in orange peel, vanilla extract, and almond extract.
7. Fold in toasted walnuts.
8. Gradually mix in the dry ingredients until just combined.
9. Cover dough and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (or up to 1 day ahead).
10. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
11. Butter and flour two baking sheets.
12. Divide dough into three equal pieces.
13. Roll each piece into a log about 1 1/2 inches in diameter on a lightly floured surface.
14. Place two logs side by side on one sheet, spacing them about 5 inches apart.
15. Place the third log on the second sheet.
16. Bake for about 20 minutes, or until the logs are light brown and have spread during baking.
17. Let cool slightly on the pan.
18. Cut each log into diagonal slices about 3/4 inch wide.
19. Turn cut sides down onto the sheets.
20. Bake for an additional 15 minutes, until golden brown.
21. Transfer biscuits to racks to cool completely.
22. Store in an airtight container.
